title: Clawhammer Banjo: Frailing, Tunes & the Old-Time Tradition
subtitle: MUS-17 · A 3-week intensive
description: Go from never having held a banjo to playing real old-time tunes with a steady, danceable groove. A playing-first course in clawhammer (frailing) technique — the strike, the thumb, the bum-ditty, hammer-ons, pull-offs, slides, and drop-thumb — built into a working repertoire (Boil Them Cabbage Down, Cripple Creek, Old Joe Clark, Shady Grove), a reliable method for learning tunes by ear, and the history of where this music comes from. Taught through daily listening, lectures, slow practice, quizzes, and projects.

lessons.title: Days 1–7: The Banjo, the Strike, the Thumb, the Bum-Ditty, Chords
Days 8–14: Hammer-ons, Pull-offs, Slides, Drop-Thumb, Tunes & Groove
Days 15–21: Alternate Tunings, Modal Tunes, Playing by Ear, Your Own Sound
